title: agrimonia striata subsp. viscidula
intro: Agrimonia striata subsp. viscidula, also known as sticky agrimony, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the rose family. Its medicinal properties make it a valuable herb, traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant is rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds, which contribute to its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Agrimonia striata subsp. viscidula can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is often used in herbal medicine to promote wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders.
